title: Understand: ecological interactions
description: Check your understanding of ecological interactions in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best phrase to fill in the blank.**

In a mutualistic interaction, the organism of one species benefits, and the organism of the other species \_\_\_\_\_\_.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: also benefits
is harmed
is not affected

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word to fill in the blank.**

In a \_\_\_\_\_\_ interaction, the organism of one species benefits, and the organism of the other species also benefits.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: mutualistic
predatory
competitive

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes a mutualistic interaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an interaction between organisms of different species where both organisms benefit
an interaction where one organism eats another organism
an interaction where two organisms compete for resources

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word to fill in the blank.**

In a \_\_\_\_\_\_ interaction, one organism hunts and eats another organism.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: mutualistic
predatory
competitive

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes a predatory interaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an interaction between organisms of different species where both organisms benefit
an interaction where one organism hunts and eats another organism
an interaction where two organisms compete for resources

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word or phrase to fill in the blank.**

In a predatory interaction, a prey organism \_\_\_\_\_\_ a predator.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: is eaten by
eats
benefits from

assessment_items.question_content:
**Choose the best word to fill in the blank.**

In a \_\_\_\_\_\_ interaction, two organisms compete for resources.

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: competitive
mutualistic
predatory

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following describes a competitive interaction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: an interaction between organisms of different species where both organisms benefit
an interaction where one organism eats another organism
an interaction where two organisms compete for resources

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which term best describes an interaction where two organisms compete for resources?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: competitive
mutualistic
predatory

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider two ecosystems: one is a mountain lake, and the other is a coral reef.

**Which statement about interactions in the lake and reef ecosystems is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The types of interactions between organisms are similar in both the lake and reef ecosystems.
Mutualistic interactions occur in the lake ecosystem but not the coral reef ecosystem.
Competitive interactions likely occur between the same species in the lake and reef ecosystems.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider two ecosystems: one is a freshwater stream, and the other is a forest.

**Which statement about interactions in the stream and forest ecosystems is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The types of interactions between organisms are similar in both the stream and forest ecosystems.
Competitive interactions occur in the stream ecosystem but not the forest ecosystem.
Predatory interactions likely occur between the same species in the stream and forest ecosystems.

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider two ecosystems: one is a grassland, and the other is a tundra.

**Which statement about interactions in the grassland and tundra ecosystems is most likely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The types of interactions between organisms are similar in both the grassland and tundra ecosystems.
Predatory interactions occur in the grassland ecosystem but not the tundra ecosystem.
Mutualistic interactions likely occur between the same species in the grassland and tundra ecosystems.
